reason: Mis-attributed provenance for the positivity and unitality of Phi. Node 1.1.1 states 'Phi is positive (monotone) and unital with Phi(I)=I, Phi(-I)=-I (GT-positive-unital)', citing the external GT-positive-unital (Idel 2013, refs/idel-2013/idel-2013.md). But these facts are NOT theorems derivable from Idel; they are DEFINITIONAL hypotheses of this proof, captured by 'def positive-unital-map' (Phi: B(H)_sa -> B(H)_sa is real-linear, positive (x>=0 => Phi(x)>=0), unital (Phi(I)=I)). Idel's Definition 2.1 (idel-2013.md:345-349) defines positivity/unitality for COMPLEX-linear maps T: M_d -> M_{d'} between matrix algebras -- a different type than the real-linear Phi: B(H)_sa -> B(H)_sa here -- and is a DEFINITION, not a structure theorem about a given Phi. This is the IDENTICAL defect already adjudicated as a valid major challenge on sibling node 1.6 (ch-79721599b24e2763, resolved), where the fix was to re-cite 'def positive-unital-map' by hypothesis instead of GT-positive-unital; node 1.1.1 was not given the same correction. The correct citation for positivity (hence monotonicity, via positivity+real-linearity) and unitality (Phi(I)=I, hence Phi(-I)=-I via real-linearity) is 'def positive-unital-map', not GT-positive-unital. Compounding this, the GT-positive-unital external's own source text asserts Idel shows 'a unital positive map on B(H)_sa is order-unit-norm contractive (||Phi||=1)', yet Idel contains ZERO occurrences of 'order unit'/'order norm'/'contractive' (grep -c = 0) and never states ||Phi||=1 -- so the external being cited is itself partly false-provenanced. Node 1.1.1's derivation is otherwise mathematically correct (||a||<=1 iff -I<=a<=I from GT-order-unit-norm/HOS:368-372 with Archimedean cone-closedness at HOS:399; monotone image; ||I||=1; ||Phi||>=||Phi(I)||=1), but it rests its positivity/unitality premises on the wrong source under the PROJECT RULE of strict provenance.
